data.summary: Added calibration/parseCsvLine.ts (RFC-4180 record parser) and parseCsvLine.test.ts covering plain, embedded-comma, escaped-quote, empty-middle-field, and empty-input cases; 5/5 tests pass.
Added calibration/topoSort.ts (Kahn topo sort, lexical tie-break, cycle throw) and topoSort.test.ts covering chain, diamond, and cycle — all 3 tests pass.
Added calibration/rpnEval.ts stack evaluator and rpnEval.test.ts with 6 passing tests covering four valid and two throwing cases.
Implemented calibration/lruCache.ts (Map-based, get+set refresh recency, capacity-bounded) with 3 passing tests covering plain eviction, get-refreshes-recency eviction, and the size bound.
Added semverCompare with semver 2.0 prerelease precedence plus a passing 5-test vitest suite.

data.summary: Added calibration/parseDuration.ts (string→ms parser) and passing vitest suite covering all required cases.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/topoSort.test.ts | 22 ++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/topoSort.ts      | 36 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 58 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/topoSort.ts exports topoSort returning a valid deterministic (lexical tie-break) ordering of all nodes; throws with "cycle" in the message on a cyclic graph: topoSort is exported and implements Kahn's algorithm: the initial ready set is .sort()ed and subsequent zero-indegree nodes are spliced in at their lexical position, keeping the ready queue sorted, so ties always break lexically and output is deterministic. All nodes are registered via seen() for both edge endpoints, so every node appears. On a cycle, out.length !== indeg.size and it throws new Error("cycle detected in graph"), which contains "cycle".
✓ calibration/topoSort.test.ts covers a linear chain, a diamond (a first / d last / predecessors before d), and a cycle throwing: The test file has exactly these three cases: a linear chain a→b→c expecting ["a","b","c"]; a diamond asserting out[0]==='a', last element 'd', b and c indexOf before d, plus the deterministic full ordering ["a","b","c","d"]; and a cyclic graph [[a,b],[b,a]] asserted to throw matching /cycle/.

1 commit(s):
calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts | 24 ++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/parseCsvLine.ts      | 24 ++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 48 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseCsvLine.ts exports parseCsvLine handling quoted fields, escaped "" quotes, embedded commas inside quotes, empty fields, and empty input. calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts covers plain, embedded-comma, escaped-quote, empty-middle-field, and empty-input cases.: parseCsvLine.ts exports parseCsvLine with a state-machine parser: inQuotes mode consumes commas verbatim (embedded commas), doubled '""' inside quotes appends a literal quote and skips ahead (escaped quotes), comma outside quotes pushes the accumulated field so 'a,,c' yields an empty middle field, and the trailing fields.push(field) makes empty input return [""]. The test file contains exactly the five required cases — plain (a,b,c), embedded comma ("a,b",c), escaped quote ("she said ""hi"""), empty middle field (a,,c), and empty input ("") — each with correct expected outputs verifiable by tracing the implementation.

1 commit(s):
calibration/rpnEval.test.ts | 12 ++++++++++++
 calibration/rpnEval.ts      | 25 +++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 37 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/rpnEval.ts exports rpnEval evaluating +,-,*,/ via a stack over ints/decimals; throws on unknown token, operand underflow, and leftover operands: rpnEval is exported, uses a number[] stack with all four operators in OPS; Number(token) accepts ints and decimals and !Number.isFinite guards unknown tokens with a throw; stack.length<2 throws underflow; stack.length!==1 after evaluation throws leftover
✓ calibration/rpnEval.test.ts covers four valid expressions and two throwing (underflow + leftover) cases: exactly four valid-expression tests (3 4 +, 3 4 + 5 *, 10 2 /, 2 3 4 * +) with correct expected values, plus one underflow throw test ("3 +") and one leftover throw test ("1 2 3 +")

1 commit(s):
calibration/semverCompare.test.ts | 27 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/semverCompare.ts      | 39 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 66 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/semverCompare.ts exports semverCompare returning -1|0|1 with correct numeric core + prerelease precedence (prerelease < release, numeric<alphanumeric, longer-outranks-shorter): semverCompare is exported and typed -1|0|1. Core is compared numerically per MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH position (so 1.2.0 > 1.1.9, 2.0.0 > 1.9.9). comparePrerelease uses '' as the release sentinel and ranks it above any prerelease (a==='' returns 1). compareIdentifier ranks all-digit identifiers numerically, numeric below alphanumeric (an && !bn → -1), and alphanumerics lexically. When all shared identifiers are equal, cmp(as.length, bs.length) makes the longer identifier set outrank the shorter prefix. All required precedence rules are implemented correctly in the diff.
✓ calibration/semverCompare.test.ts covers core ordering, equality, prerelease-vs-release, and two prerelease-identifier cases: Tests cover core ordering (1.0.0<1.0.1, 1.2.0>1.1.9, 2.0.0>1.9.9), equality (1.0.0==1.0.0), prerelease-vs-release in both directions (1.0.0-alpha < 1.0.0), and two prerelease-identifier cases: numeric-vs-alphanumeric (alpha.1 < alpha.beta) and longer-set-outranks-prefix (alpha.1 > alpha). Assertions match semver 2.0 precedence.

data.summary: Added calibration/lruCache.ts (Map-backed LRU, recency on get+set, in-place update no growth) and lruCache.test.ts covering plain eviction, get-refreshes-recency eviction, and the size bound — all 3 pass.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/parseDuration.test.ts | 34 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/parseDuration.ts      | 34 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 68 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseDuration.ts exports parseDuration returning ms; handles ms/s/m/h/d, multi-unit, case-insensitive, whitespace; throws on empty/unknown-unit/negative: Exported parseDuration returns a number in ms via UNIT_MS covering ms/s/m/h/d. The sticky regex loop sums multiple number+unit parts (multi-unit), the `i` flag plus toLowerCase() on the unit gives case-insensitivity, and trim() plus the explicit space/tab skip between parts handles whitespace. Empty/whitespace-only input throws on the `!src` check after trim; unknown units ("5x", "h") leave `pos !== src.length` and throw the unparseable error (a failed sticky exec is caught by the pos-vs-length check, with the lastIndex-reset pitfall handled via the tracked `pos`); negative values match via `-?` in the regex and throw on the `value < 0` check. Traced "90s"→90000, "1h30m"→5400000, "2d 4h"→187200000, "1H30M"→5400000 — all consistent with the tests.
✓ calibration/parseDuration.test.ts covers 90s, 1h30m, 500ms, 2d, case-insensitivity, and two throwing cases: Tests assert parseDuration("90s")===90_000, "500ms"===500, "2d"===172_800_000, "1h30m"===5_400_000, case-insensitivity via "1H30M", and more than two throwing cases: empty/whitespace input, unknown unit "5x", missing number "h", and negative "-5s".

1 commit(s):
calibration/lruCache.test.ts | 36 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/lruCache.ts      | 31 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 67 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/lruCache.ts exports lruCache with get/set/size: Diff creates calibration/lruCache.ts exporting function lruCache<K,V>(capacity) returning an object with get, set, and size methods.
✓ capacity-bounded: set() checks store.size > capacity after insert and deletes the first Map key (LRU); size can never exceed capacity after any set.
✓ recency refreshed by BOTH get and set: get() on a hit calls touch() (delete + re-insert, moving the key to most-recent), and set() always routes through touch(); Map insertion order encodes recency correctly.
✓ in-place update doesn't grow size: touch() deletes the existing key before re-inserting, so updating a present key leaves store.size unchanged; test asserts size()===1 after two sets of 'a'.
✓ test covers plain eviction: First test fills capacity 2, adds a third key, and asserts the untouched oldest key 'a' is evicted while 'b' and 'c' survive.
✓ test covers get-refreshes-recency eviction: Second test gets 'a' before inserting 'c' and asserts 'b' (now LRU) is evicted instead of 'a' — this fails if get() did not refresh recency.
✓ test covers the size bound: Third test asserts size()===1 after an in-place update and size()===2 after inserting 4 keys into a capacity-2 cache.
